reasoning.typography: Georgia serif for body, Helvetica sans for headings — a classic pairing with character, though not groundbreaking. The italic emphasis in body copy adds texture. Safe but effective.
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong headline dominance on each essay title. Clear visual weight differentiation between title, subheads, and body. The bulleted subheads work hard to create scannable structure.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Intensely personal voice baked into the IA itself — these aren't generic service offerings, they're opinionated essays with deliberately provocative titles. Couldn't swap this for another consultant's site.
reasoning.color: Disciplined blue accent for links/CTA, dark background, restrained palette. The blue subscribe button is the only saturated color moment. Very controlled.
reasoning.density: Confident content density — the homepage IS the content. No hero images, no testimonial carousels, just essay titles and descriptions. Unusual restraint for 2024.
reasoning.voice: Perfect IA/content fit. The structure is 'here are my thoughts' rather than 'About/Services/Contact'. The essay titles ('You don't need to work on hard problems') do all the positioning work. No stencil detected.

winning_patterns: Homepage as direct content index — no marketing theater, just the actual work
Essay titles as positioning statements — each headline is a thesis
Bullet-point essay summaries create scannable density without walls of text
Dark mode as default shows conviction rather than trend-following
Subscribe CTA integrated naturally rather than modal popup assault
anti_patterns: The footer SVG wave illustration is the only decorative element and feels unnecessary
Link underlines in blue (#47e) create slight visual noise at scale
Georgia body text is safe/academic rather than distinctive
Top nav is almost invisible — could be more confident
Email input placeholder 'you@example.com' is stale web convention
